libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use Conscrypt explicitly in tests rather than assuming it is the default JCA/JSSE provider: call Conscrypt.checkAvailability(), create Provider provider = Conscrypt.newProvider(), and pass that provider to JCA/JSSE factories such as SSLContext.getInstance("TLS", provider), or temporarily install it with Security.insertProviderAt in test setup and restore providers afterward. Preserve the existing Java/native test JVM arguments that open java.base/java.lang, java.base/java.net, and java.base/sun.nio.ch when exercising socket and platform internals. No org.conscrypt.native.workdir setting is needed unless the test environment's default temp directory is noexec or unavailable.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8879 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.conscrypt:conscrypt-openjdk-uber:1.4.1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The reported native-image runtime failure appears tied to Conscrypt JNI/native-image/JDK internals rather than to a missing external service or dependency.

Conscrypt extracts and loads a bundled native library at runtime; unusual temp-directory permissions can require org.conscrypt.native.workdir, but there is no evidence that the standard Ubuntu CI environment needs it.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed: the uber artifact has no declared runtime dependencies and bundles the platform native Conscrypt JNI libraries. Meaningful OpenJDK coverage does require test-code setup to create or install the Conscrypt security provider explicitly.
